blocks.content: This is the introduction paragraph that comes before any sections.

terms.thai: การสังเคราะห์ด้วยแสง
terms.definition: The process by which plants convert sunlight into energy

terms.thai: คลอโรฟิลล์
terms.definition: The green pigment in plants that captures sunlight
A type of sugar that plants produce during photosynthesis

blocks.title: Reading Passage
blocks.content:
Plants are amazing organisms that can create their own food through a process called photosynthesis. During this process, plants use sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide to produce glucose and oxygen.

The leaves of a plant contain tiny structures called chloroplasts, which contain chlorophyll. This green pigment absorbs sunlight and uses its energy to power the chemical reactions of photosynthesis.

steps.instruction: Examine your plants carefully using the magnifying glass
subSteps: Look at the color and shape of the leaves
Count the number of leaves on each plant

steps.instruction: Place one plant in direct sunlight
Place the other plant in a dark closet
Observe both plants daily for one week
subSteps: Record your observations in your science notebook
Draw pictures of any changes you see

blocks.alt: Student observing plants in science class

blocks.content: What do you think will happen to each plant? Why do plants need sunlight to survive?
